Result of Annual General Meeting

eEnergy (AIM: EAAS), which designs and delivers energy-saving and energy-generating solutions to its customers reducing their costs and mitigating the impact of future increases in energy costs, confirms that all resolutions proposed at its Annual General Meeting ("AGM"), held earlier today, were duly passed.

Further details of each of the resolutions are set out in the Notice of General Meeting, which can be found on the Company's website:

As previously announced, following conclusion of the AGM Andrew Lawley and Dr Nigel Burton have stepped down from the Board. Gary Worby will step down from the Board on 30 June 2026. John Samuel, independent non-executive director, has moved to the Chairman role.
Change of Registered Office
The Company also notifies that the address of its registered office has changed with effect from today to:
Mary Sheridan House, 11-19 St Thomas Street, London, SE1 9RY.
For further information, please visit

About eEnergy Group plc
eEnergy (AIM: EAAS) is a UK-based Energy-as-a-Service (EaaS) provider, funding and delivering energy-saving and energy-generating solutions across multi-site public sector and commercial portfolios-helping customers cut energy waste, reduce operating costs, and improve building resilience with zero upfront cost.
eEnergy delivers four core solutions:
·
Reduce
: LED lighting and controls
· Generate
: Solar PV (rooftop, ground mount, and carport)
·
Store
: Battery storage (store onsite generation and reduce peak-time import costs)
·
Charge
: EV charging infrastructure and management
Projects are funded through dedicated third-party debt facilities, including up to £100m of project funding via eEnergy's partnership with Redaptive.
eEnergy's routes to market include direct sales, public sector frameworks, tenders, and strategic partnerships. The Group holds positions on five major procurement frameworks; CCS (Crown Commercial Service), LASER, Lexica/NHS London, NHS Commercial Solutions Framework, and Proactis (YPO) and is an Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) approved EV charge point installer.
The Group has delivered over 1,200 projects and has installed c.590,000 LEDs, improving learning environments for c.520,000 students.
eEnergy is a market leader in the education sector and has been awarded the London Stock Exchange's Green Economy Mark. The Company is also recognised in the 2025 UK Fast Growth 50 Index within the Fastest Growing Green Firms 2025 list, and holds an EcoVadis Bronze Medal with a score of 61/100, placing it in the top third of more than 130,000 organisations assessed globally.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
22 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1735J
eEnergy Group PLC
22 June 2026
22 June 2026
eEnergy Group plc
("eEnergy", "the Company" or "the Group")
Trading Update
eEnergy (AIM: EAAS), which designs and delivers energy-saving and energy-generating solutions to its customers reducing their costs and mitigating the impact of future increases in energy costs, will hold its Annual General Meeting ("AGM") at 11am at Fieldfisher, Riverbank House, 2 Swan Lane, London, EC4R 3TT on 25 June 2026. The Company also provides a trading update.
Ahead of the AGM, the Group's Chair, Andrew Lawley, has issued the following statement:
"Following the appointment of John Gahan as Interim CEO, we have conducted a detailed review of the pipeline of potential sales opportunities. Consequently, the Board now believes that investment grade opportunities equivalent to £66m more fairly reflect the level of live opportunities that the business could potentially convert into revenue in the short to medium term.
"John Gahan was appointed as the Interim CEO in May 2026 and commenced a restructuring and cost saving exercise to simplify the reporting structure and right size the cost base. In total, this exercise is expected to reduce annual operating costs by almost a third and generate annualised total savings of circa £2.0m in FY26 (FY25 operating costs of circa £6.3m). The cost reduction exercise is expected to improve H2-26 Adjusted EBITDA by circa £1.0m. H1-26 will include an exceptional restructuring charge of circa £0.5m in respect of the restructuring exercise implemented in June 2026.
"The Board now expects H1-26 Revenue of circa £22.0m (H1-25: £10.1m) and H1-26 Adjusted EBITDA of circa £1.2m* (H1-25: £0.5m*).
"Following the detailed review and significant reduction in pipeline revenue, the Board now expects FY26 Revenue to be circa £32.0m (previously £38.0m; FY25: £19.0m) with FY26 Adjusted EBITDA of £1.7m* (previously £4.5m*; FY25: £2.2m*)."
Notice of interim results
The Board expects to announce the Interim Results for the six months ending 30 June 2026 on or around 30 July 2026.
Notes
*Adjusted EBITDA is stated post central costs and before share-based payments and exceptional items (including redundancy costs and payments in lieu of notice) which are those items which, in the opinion of the Directors, should be excluded to provide a consistent and comparable view of the underlying performance of the Group's ongoing business.
The person responsible for arranging for the release of this announcement on behalf of eEnergy is John Gahan, Interim Chief Executive Officer.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014, as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, as amended.
For further information, please visit
www.eenergy.com
or contact:
eEnergy Group plc

latest.title: Publication of AR, Notice of AGM and Board Update
latest.content:
1 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4130G
eEnergy Group PLC
01 June 2026
1 June 2026
eEnergy Group plc
("eEnergy", "the Company" or "the Group")
Publication of FY2025 Annual Report and Accounts,
Notice of AGM and proposed Board change
eEnergy (AIM: EAAS), an Energy-as-a-Service provider delivering energy infrastructure upgrades across multi-site portfolios with zero upfront cost for its customers, announces that its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025 have today been published, together with a notice ("
Notice
") convening the Company's annual general meeting ("
AGM
").
The AGM is be held at the offices of Fieldfisher LLP at Riverbank House, 2 Swan Lane, London, EC4R 3TT at 11:00 am on 25 June 2026. The Notice, together with proxy forms, will also be published on the Company's website.
Copies of the Company's Annual Report and Accounts and Notice of AGM are available on the Company's website at
www.eenergy.com
.
Andrew Lawley, Chairman and non-executive director, has informed the Board that he does not wish to stand for re-election at the AGM and intends to step down from the Board at the conclusion of the AGM. The Board thanks Andrew for his service and wishes him well in his future endeavours. John Samuel, currently non-executive director, will move to the Chairman role following the AGM.
For further information, please visit

latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
18 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6898E
eEnergy Group PLC
18 May 2026
18 May 2026
eEnergy Group plc
("eEnergy", "the Company" or "the Group")
Directorate Change
eEnergy (AIM: EAAS), an Energy-as-a-Service provider delivering funded energy infrastructure upgrades across multi-site portfolios, announces that Harvey Sinclair has stepped down as Chief Executive Officer and as a Director of the Company by mutual agreement with the Board and with immediate effect. Harvey will leave the Group at the end of May to pursue other opportunities. As part of the agreed transition arrangements, Harvey has agreed to waive his outstanding 28,080,000 non-EMI share options.
John Gahan, Chief Financial Officer takes over Harvey's responsibilities as Chief Executive Officer on an interim basis until a replacement Chief Executive Officer can be appointed. A further announcement will be made in due course.
Andrew Lawley, Chairman of eEnergy commented:
"On behalf of the Board, I would like to thank Harvey for his service to the Company. We will make a further announcement about his replacement in due course."
For further information, please visit

company_30s.what_they_do: EAAS builds and supplies solar power systems and electric‑vehicle charging stations for commercial and public sites. It designs, installs and maintains renewable energy infrastructure, often partnering with large organisations such as the NHS.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The world is moving toward clean energy, and governments are demanding greener transport hubs and office parks. EAAS sits in the middle of that shift, offering turnkey solar panels and charging points that help businesses meet carbon targets and reduce operating costs.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still scaling. It has a handful of contracts and a recent loan to keep the pipeline moving, but cash burn is high and it has not yet posted sustained profits. Its share price is very low, reflecting the risk that investors see in a small, still‑growth‑phase renewable player.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy on the board‑room front, shuffling directors and updating shareholders on results. That shows a willingness to adapt governance to attract new investors.
Operationally, EAAS is clinching new solar agreements and an EV‑charging project with the NHS – a high‑profile client that signals credibility.
Financially, the firm is raising capital through a loan, options and a new funding solution, indicating it needs fresh cash to support its contract pipeline.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bullish chatter in a quiet market
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Investors are excited about the NHS contract and the steady stream of solar deals. They see the company as a niche renewable provider poised for growth.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Critics worry that the company’s cash burn is unsustainable and that frequent board changes hint at strategic uncertainty.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation has stayed upbeat; no major negative voices have emerged, so the mood remains cautiously optimistic.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – there’s no mainstream buzz, which means a good catalyst could catch everyone off guard.

near_term_catalysts: Q2 trading update: any revenue or margin improvement will lift confidence.
Announcement of a new solar contract or extension of the NHS charging project will validate the pipeline.
If the loan is fully drawn or the funding solution is used, it signals confidence from lenders and investors.
A surge in trading volume on the day of a positive RNS would confirm market interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear earnings beat, a confirmed new contract, or a jump in share volume that breaks the low‑price range. A sudden drop in board turnover or a steady cash flow statement would also be reassuring.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is still in the early part of a recovery cycle – the market has already taken a deep hit, but the price is still far below historic highs. A single positive announcement could start a chain reaction.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company has a proven renewable niche, a high‑profile NHS contract, and a fresh loan that can fund growth. With a 70‑95% drop in price, a rebound to even half of the previous peak would more than double the investment. The system’s track record shows that deep crashes often lead to outsized bounces.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure additional contracts or if cash burn continues unchecked, the price could fall further or the firm could face delisting. A realistic downside is a further 20‑30% decline before any recovery.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: up to 200% if the share rebounds to the mid‑range of its historic high. Potential downside: 20‑30% if fundamentals don’t improve. The asymmetry favours the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when price touches the 5p support level and volume spikes, place a stop just below that support (around 4p). Use a position size that keeps risk below 2% of the portfolio, scaling out as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the Q2 update or a new contract announcement. If volume exceeds 200% of average daily volume or the share price climbs 10% on news, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: Only if the company announces a bankruptcy filing or is forced to delist. Currently no such terminal risk is evident.

ai_insights.bottom_line: EAAS sits at a deep, silent crash with a renewable‑energy pipeline and a fresh loan. A single positive earnings or contract announcement could spark a rebound, offering high upside against modest downside.

Historical data shows 2 rallies averaging 187% upside. Current position: +94.5%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
